title: Xenial Malaysian Beef Stew
description: A rich, fluffy, comforting Malaysian beef stew with aromatic cooking wine, reduced sauce, and sugarfree sweetness.
instructions: Heat a Dutch oven over high heat and sear the beef cubes in batches until browned on all sides, then remove and set aside.
In the same pot, add a splash of cooking wine to deglaze, scraping up brown bits, and let it evaporate for 2 minutes.
Add the sliced shallots, minced garlic, and ginger to the pot and sauté until fragrant, about 3 minutes.
Return the beef to the pot and pour in the beef broth, soy sauce, tamarind paste, monk fruit sweetener, and coconut milk.
Stir well, bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer uncovered for 90 minutes, uncovered to thicken the stew.
During the last 15 minutes, add the sliced chilies and a handful of fresh coriander leaves to the simmering stew.
Taste and adjust seasoning with extra soy sauce or monk fruit sweetener if needed, then continue to simmer until the beef is tender and the sauce has reduced to a creamy consistency.
Remove the pot from heat and let the stew rest for 5 minutes, allowing flavors to meld.
Ladle the stew into bowls and garnish with additional coriander leaves before serving hot.
Serve with steamed jasmine rice or flatbread to soak up the rich sauce.
